title: Scan an image's layers for secrets and bloat
description: Inspect built image layers for embedded credentials, leftover build tooling, and oversized layers, reporting each with the originating instruction.
body: Use when validating a built image before publish. Actions: read the image history/layers, flag any layer containing credential-like strings or .env/key files, identify leftover compilers or package caches in the runtime stage, and report oversized layers with the Dockerfile instruction that created them. Do not treat a deleted-in-a-later-layer secret as gone — earlier layers still hold it. This skill exposes capability only and must not push or modify the image without runtime permission. Validation signals include a clean secret scan across all layers and a runtime stage with no build toolchain. Risk boundary: a secret found in any layer is a disclosure; recommend rebuilding without it and rotating the credential rather than layer-squashing to hide it, and escalate the exposure.
